hypotheses.name: Numbered 1..5 with rank-weighted typographic scale
hypotheses.rationale: Retained concept from v1 numbered-priority-list. v1 used constant 28pt for all 5 numerals — no rank scaling, no hierarchy delivered. v2 corrects: rank-weighted scale 36/30/24/20/16pt (geometric series, smallest 16pt clears the 10pt body floor).
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport — eye lands on #1 first by design.

hypotheses.name: Manifesto single statement in registered Vollkorn Italic
hypotheses.rationale: Retained concept from v1 manifesto-single-statement. v1 specified Vollkorn Black (non-italic) which is NOT in shared/ci.yml::fonts — only Vollkorn Black Italic is. v2 uses the registered italic face and drops the v1 footer that contradicted single-statement ownership.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Polarising — the reader either reads the whole panel or skips it entirely.

hypotheses.name: Three items separated by thin Dunkelgrün rules
hypotheses.rationale: Retained concept from v1 asymmetric-editorial-rules. v1 used 5 rows × 22mm = 110mm packed into 130mm panel (~15% whitespace, below the hcd #3 30% floor). v2 reduces to 3 items, leaving ~49% body-area whitespace while preserving the thin-rule separator and intentional right-third whitespace.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal among design-literate raters; editorial composition.

hypotheses.name: Two-tier hierarchy with one privileged item on Dunkelgrün block
hypotheses.rationale: The five even-weight slogans are flat; the eye lands nowhere. Privilege one cornerstone slogan by setting it on a full-width Dunkelgrün block in white reverse contrast at display size, leaving three peer slogans below in Dunkelgrün-on-Hellgrün at body weight. The accent is the brand's own text-on-green pairing, not Gelb, so the move stays inside the envelope.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport (clear primary message), polarising appeal (some readers will resent the implied ranking).

hypotheses.name: Replace list with one cohesive prose paragraph
hypotheses.rationale: A list invites scanning and dismissal; prose forces a reading commitment. Set one continuous 11pt Gotham paragraph that argues for the candidate's plan as connected reasoning, not as bullets. Trades scannability for narrative pull and a more adult register.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Lower appeal among quick-scanners, higher transport among readers who actually engage — and a clearer signal about whether Schlagwort lists are doing the work the team thinks they are.

hypotheses.name: Three items with categorical eyebrow above each
hypotheses.rationale: Cut to three items but signpost each with a small Gotham Bold all-caps eyebrow (KLIMA / WOHNEN / BILDUNG) sitting above a single short body sentence. The eyebrow turns each item from slogan into a labelled commitment; body stays at 10pt minimum to respect the floor. Hierarchy now operates inside each row, not just between rows.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport thanks to topical labels giving non-engaged readers an entry point; appeal trade depends on whether the categorical framing reads bureaucratic.

hypotheses.name: Three horizontal bands, central Dunkelgrün privileges cornerstone
hypotheses.rationale: Stack three full-width horizontal bands — Hellgrün top, Dunkelgrün centre, Hellgrün bottom — each carrying one slogan. The central Dunkelgrün band reverses contrast (white display text on Dunkelgrün, brand-compliant) and is given roughly 1.6× the height of its neighbours, making it the optical anchor without using Gelb or breaking text_on_green.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal (rhythmic, confident composition), mixed transport — readers may absorb the central message but skip the supporting bands.

hypotheses.name: Vollkorn Black Italic cornerstone with a real 2.5× jump
hypotheses.rationale: One cornerstone phrase set in registered Vollkorn Black Italic at 36pt; three supporting items in Gotham at 14pt — a clean 2.57× headline-jump that satisfies the floor instead of fudging it. The italic cornerstone shifts the panel from political-slogan register into editorial / personal commitment.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal with design-literate readers (editorial register reads thoughtful), risk that voters experience italic display as soft or insufficiently emphatic.

hypotheses.name: Each of three slogans tagged with caps category eyebrow
hypotheses.rationale: Three slogans each preceded by a 9pt Gotham Bold all-caps category tag in Dunkelgrün, sitting flush-left above the slogan with 4mm baseline gap. Hierarchy operates by typographic weight and case, not colour or scale jump, so the panel stays quiet but legible at distance and the category framing aids transport.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport for cold readers (categories give them a frame), middling appeal — the move is informational rather than emotive.

hypotheses.name: 60/40 editorial column split — promises left, identity right
hypotheses.rationale: Break the panel into a 60/40 vertical split. Left column holds three promises stacked with generous baseline rhythm; right column carries the candidate's name + role on a flush-top Dunkelgrün rule, then drops to whitespace. The asymmetric grid borrows newspaper-spread logic and forces the eye through promises before identity.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal for readers who value editorial polish, lower for readers who expect symmetric political layouts; transport rises if the role label gives the promises authorship.

hypotheses.name: Two display statements as call-and-response
hypotheses.rationale: Replace the list with two statements arranged as dialogue: an upper statement at display size posing the stakes (e.g. "Wie weiter im Kreis?") and a lower statement, separated by a 4mm Dunkelgrün rule, delivering the answer (e.g. "Mit klarem Kurs."). Density drops from five to two, and the second statement carries the commitment via call-and-response rhythm rather than bullet-list parity.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal (rhythm, confident pause), lower transport on detail (only one promise lands, the rest is implied).

hypotheses.name: Resident quote + candidate reply, single type family
hypotheses.rationale: Open with a verbatim resident quote in Gotham Italic at body size, then a candidate reply in Gotham Bold at 1.4× body — both inside the Gotham family so the panel stays within the 2-family floor. The quote-then-reply structure reframes the candidate as listener-first; the family-restraint avoids v1's third-family violation.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal among reader-empathy voters; transport hinges on whether the chosen quote does diagnostic work or reads like a prop.

hypotheses.name: Portrait fills panel, single hand-set commitment overlays in Dunkelgrün block
hypotheses.rationale: Wildcard: drop the Schlagwort list entirely. The panel becomes a face_crop ≥60% portrait of the candidate; a single short commitment phrase sits in a Dunkelgrün rectangular plate anchored to the lower third (text_on_green compliant, plate avoids partial-overlap of the face frame). The piece stops being a list of promises and becomes a face attached to one sentence — directions the team historically resists for being too personality-driven.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Polarising on appeal (high among voters who respond to candidates as people, low among programmatic voters), strong transport for the single attached commitment, near-zero transport for everything else.

hypotheses.name: Manifesto single pledge
hypotheses.rationale: Collapse the peer list into one dominant sentence set in Vollkorn Black Italic, with only a short supporting line in Gotham beneath it. The panel stops behaving like a checklist and instead reads as a single political promise with clear emotional entry.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely high appeal through force and memorability, with transport split depending on whether raters prefer focus over coverage.

hypotheses.name: Three pillars with explainer lines
hypotheses.rationale: Reduce the five slogans to three priorities and attach one short explanatory sentence to each, keeping body text safely above 10pt. The structure turns abstract claims into a ranked argument and gives each item enough room to mean something.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely strongest on transport, with appeal dependent on whether voters reward clarity over punch.

hypotheses.name: Editorial left column with rules
hypotheses.rationale: Anchor the content in a left-hand text column with thin Dunkelgrün separators and preserve a deliberately open right side instead of centering everything. The composition creates tension and makes the empty space feel intentional rather than leftover.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely higher appeal among design-sensitive raters, with transport improving if the asymmetry helps scanning.

hypotheses.name: Ranked numerals with descending scale
hypotheses.rationale: Keep all five items but make the ranking explicit through oversized numerals that visibly step down from 1 to 5, while one numeral takes the accent color. This preserves breadth yet replaces flat equality with an unmistakable reading order.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely balanced on appeal and transport because it keeps information volume while adding a clear entry point.

hypotheses.name: First-person compact paragraph
hypotheses.rationale: Replace the list entirely with one short first-person paragraph that reads as a compact commitment rather than five separate claims. The form asks voters to process a coherent stance instead of hopping across equal-weight fragments.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely moderate appeal but improved message coherence if raters respond to a more personal contract tone.

hypotheses.name: Cornerstone banner with minor items
hypotheses.rationale: Give one priority a full-width top banner and place the remaining items below as smaller supporting lines in a secondary block. The anatomy creates a clear leader-with-supporters structure without forcing the supporting text below minimum size.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely strong on transport because the main promise is unmistakable, with appeal helped by the more decisive structure.

hypotheses.name: Accent chip with open field
hypotheses.rationale: Concentrate visual energy into one compact accent-backed chip for the key phrase and let the rest of the panel breathe with sparse secondary text. The design tests whether one small privileged signal can outperform a fully populated slogan field.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely higher appeal through restraint, with transport depending on whether the privileged chip carries enough of the message alone.

hypotheses.name: Ballot cross as text container
hypotheses.rationale: Use the Wahlkreuz not as a small brand sign-off but as the structural text container itself, with the pledge occupying one arm and support lines occupying the other. It is an unusually literal form move that could reveal whether voters respond to a more symbolic, campaign-object composition than the team would normally permit.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely polarising on appeal but valuable for signal because it tests a radically different memory structure.
